subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle v(t) Graph with Areas 8, 3, 2 on [0,3],[3,5],[5,6], x(0)=-2 — Farthest Left, IVT Count, Speed & Acceleration
stimulus: A particle moves along the $x$-axis so that its velocity at time $t$, for $0 \le t \le 6$, is given by a differentiable function $v$ whose graph is shown above. The velocity is $0$ at $t = 0$, $t = 3$, and $t = 5$, and the graph has horizontal tangents at $t = 1$ and $t = 4$. The areas of the regions bounded by the $t$-axis and the graph of $v$ on the intervals $[0,3]$, $[3,5]$, and $[5,6]$ are $8$, $3$, and $2$, respectively. At time $t = 0$, the particle is at $x = -2$.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 6$, find both the time and the position of the particle when the particle is farthest to the left. Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x(3) = -2 - 8 = -10$; $x(6) = -9$. Farthest left at $t = 3$, position $x = -10$.

scoring_points.description: Identifies $t = 3$ as a candidate (since $v < 0$ on $(0,3)$ and $v > 0$ on $(3,5)$, position is minimized at $t = 3$)
Correct conclusion: the particle is farthest left at time $t = 3$ when its position is $x = -10$

required_elements: Correct conclusion: the particle is farthest left at time $t = 3$ when its position is $x = -10$

parts.prompt: For how many values of $t$, where $0 \le t \le 6$, is the particle at $x = -8$? Explain your reasoning.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x(0)=-2$, $x(3)=-10$, $x(5)=-7$, $x(6)=-9$. IVT gives 3 values of $t$ where $x(t) = -8$.

scoring_points.description: Describes motion: particle moves continuously from $-2$ to $-10$ (passing $-8$), then $-10$ to $-7$ (passing $-8$), then $-7$ to $-9$ (passing $-8$)

required_elements: Describes motion: particle moves continuously from $-2$ to $-10$ (passing $-8$), then $-10$ to $-7$ (passing $-8$), then $-7$ to $-9$ (passing $-8$)

scoring_points.description: Correct conclusion: by the Intermediate Value Theorem, the particle is at $x = -8$ for exactly $3$ values of $t$

required_elements: Correct conclusion: by the Intermediate Value Theorem, the particle is at $x = -8$ for exactly $3$ values of $t$

parts.prompt: On the interval $2 < t < 3$, is the speed of the particle increasing or decreasing? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Speed is decreasing because $v < 0$ and $v$ is increasing (acceleration and velocity have opposite signs) on $(2,3)$.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer with reason: speed is decreasing on $2 < t < 3$ because on this interval $v(t) < 0$ and $v$ is increasing (i.e., $v$ and $a$ have opposite signs)

required_elements: Correct answer with reason: speed is decreasing on $2 < t < 3$ because on this interval $v(t) < 0$

parts.prompt: During what time intervals, if any, is the acceleration of the particle negative? Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Acceleration is negative on $0 < t < 1$ and $4 < t < 6$ because velocity is decreasing on these intervals.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer: acceleration is negative on $(0,1)$ and $(4,6)$

required_elements: Correct answer: acceleration is negative on $(0,1)$

scoring_points.description: Justification: velocity is decreasing on these intervals (horizontal tangents at $t=1$ and $t=4$ confirm sign changes of $v'$)

required_elements: Justification: velocity is decreasing on these intervals (horizontal tangents at $t=1$
